page.title: Business Process Management
flowTab.elementsTableSubtitle: Click on Application, Data Object, IT Component, or TCode cells to edit. Automation is auto-determined from the BPMN element type.
flowTab.automatedTooltip: Auto-determined from BPMN element type (serviceTask, scriptTask, businessRuleTask = Yes)
flowTab.appliesToLane: Applies to all steps in lane “{{lane}}” — changing or removing it updates the whole lane
flowTab.submitDraftToPublish: Submit a draft for approval to publish it here.
flowTab.createDraftToPublish: Create a draft, then submit it for approval to publish.
flowTab.preLinkElementsSubtitle: Pre-link applications, data objects, IT components, and TCodes before publishing. These links will be applied when this version is approved.
flowTab.noNamedElements: No named elements found in this draft.
flowTab.draftElementLinkFailed: Failed to update draft element link
flowTab.archivedOnOriginallyApproved: on {{archivedDate}}. Originally approved by {{name}} on {{approvedDate}} — Revision {{revision}}
flowTab.confirmSubmitDescription: This draft will be sent to the business process owner for approval. You will not be able to edit it while it is pending.
flowTab.confirmApproveDescription: This will publish the process flow. The current published version (if any) will be archived. This action cannot be undone.
flowTab.confirmRejectDescription: This will return the draft to the author for revision. They will be notified.
flowTab.confirmDeleteDescription: This will permanently delete the draft. This cannot be undone.
assessment.title: Process Assessments
assessment.noAssessments: No assessments yet. Create one to start tracking process maturity.
assessment.helpIntro: Use this guide to score processes consistently. Each dimension is rated on a 1–5 scale where 1 = ad-hoc / poor and 5 = optimised / excellent.
assessment.helpEfficiencyDesc: How economically the process uses resources (time, cost, effort) to produce its outputs. Look at cycle time, cost per transaction, rework, and waste. References: Lean / Six Sigma, APQC PCF.
assessment.helpEffectivenessDesc: How well the process achieves its intended business outcome and meets stakeholder expectations. Look at quality, customer satisfaction, first-time-right, KPI attainment. References: Balanced Scorecard, OKRs.
assessment.helpComplianceDesc: How well the process adheres to internal policy, regulatory requirements, and audit controls. Look at audit findings, control gaps, segregation of duties. References: COBIT, ISO 9001, SOX, GDPR.
assessment.helpAutomationDesc: Degree to which process steps are executed by systems vs. humans. Consider straight-through processing, RPA coverage, manual touch-points, exception rate. References: BPMN automation, Hyperautomation.
assessment.helpOverallDesc: Holistic process maturity score. Use CMMI-style anchors: 1 Initial, 2 Managed, 3 Defined, 4 Quantitatively Managed, 5 Optimising.
assessment.helpBestPractice1: Anchor scores to evidence (KPIs, audit results, observations) — not opinion.
assessment.helpBestPractice2: Assess each dimension independently before deriving the overall score.
assessment.helpBestPractice3: Use a calibration session with at least one business and one IT stakeholder.
assessment.helpBestPractice4: Re-assess on a regular cadence (quarterly or post-change) to track trends.
assessment.helpBestPractice5: Tie low scores to concrete improvement actions in the Initiative backlog.
assessment.helpReferences: APQC Process Classification Framework, CMMI, Lean Six Sigma, BPMN 2.0, COBIT, ISO 9001, TOGAF Business Architecture.
